summary: Rauch 说，v0 现在不只是做 UI 了，也开始做 physics。Karpathy 则提到，GitHub Gists 的评论区出奇地好。Levie 认为，AI 提升效率，带来的不是更少工作，而是更多工作。Tan 则称这是 open source 的黄金时代。

builderInsights.title: Guillermo Rauch
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: v0 现在不只是做 UI，还能做物理模拟了
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他演示了 v0 生成一个超逼真的月球旗帜模拟。然后再喂给它 C++ 和 Rust 的参考资料，它就变得更聪明了。更夸张的是，它还自己做了优化，把物理计算挪到 Web Worker 里。这对 Vercel 的 tooling stack 来说，算是很漂亮的一次秀肌肉。

tweets.text:
The @vercel team is always listening and engaging with customers on @x. We shape the product as a result of these conversations.

Vercel gives us the velocity to ship, X gives us a nonstop stream of user feedback. Thanks @simhskal & @riyvir! https://t.co/8FxoT66mfv

Created a flag on the moon based with hyper-realistic physics with @v0: https://t.co/XyE8a2xBEB.

The flag is simulated as a grid of particles connected by springs (cloth simulation). It's influenced by gravity, wind, air resistance.

The initial approach v0 one-shotted was quite simplistic, but giving it references to more sophisticated approaches in C++ and Rust quickly improved it. It was mind-blowing it to see it deeply understand the physics and port new ideas across languages.

It simulated a real sun for lighting, and procedurally generated moon-like terrain with craters. I found a sick free texture on Reddit that I uploaded and it layered on top.

Another epic moment was watching it improve performance. It seamlessly moved the physics simulation to a Web Worker. (Kinda cool to see given we just improved support for them in Turbopack 😉)

builderInsights.authorBio: I like to train large deep neural nets. Previously Director of AI @ Tesla, founding team @ OpenAI, PhD @ Stanford.
builderInsights.title: Andrej Karpathy
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: GitHub Gists 的评论意外地很有质量
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，gist 里的评论比想象中更有帮助，也更建设性，AI 味儿没那么重。他在想，这到底是社区氛围、markdown 格式，还是激励机制本来就没那么强。总之，他现在更想多发点 gists 了。

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI 效率会带来更多工作，而不是更少
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他认为，AI agent 不会把大多数行业的需求打没，反而会把需求做大。代码更多了，就需要更多 automation、更多 security、更多 compliance、更多 media、更多 legal work。甚至 healthcare ops 也会变多，因为 scheduling 变便宜了。核心观点很简单：效率会释放二阶需求。所以，AI 对 jobs 的影响，远比“会不会毁掉工作”这种说法复杂得多。

tweets.text:
There are far more categories where AI agents making things more efficient will induce demand for that skill than spaces where agents eliminate the work. This is why the AI jobs predictions will not play out as advertised. 

AI making it easy to produce more code will mean we start to apply code to far more parts of our businesses. We will build automation and software for things that wouldn’t have made sense before. Marketing automation, client onboarding, modernizing old systems, doing far more research on existing data, and more. More engineers.

Far more software will mean vastly more security risks. This will mean far more people thinking through system security, compliance, and governance. This used to be primarily manual and only large companies could afford this work. AI will make it so more companies care about this (and maybe can do something about it), causing more security roles.

AI will also lower the cost of a bunch of previously relatively niche or harder to access categories of work. Companies will now be doing 10X more with video and graphics, and will need people to manage that work. More media. We’re going to have a near unlimited set of legal challenges in a world of AI as AI helps write even more bespoke and complicated legal docs. More lawyers. 

Then there’s the impact of AI efficiency on non-office worker jobs. Talked to a customer that said they’re going to make scheduling medical appointments and getting referrals so efficient the next problem will be there will be no booking time slots available. More healthcare. Many industries will have this same dynamic play out.

The examples are endless once you start to think through second order effects of agents making work more efficient.

builderInsights.authorBio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop—Loves using emdashes
builderInsights.title: Garry Tan
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，开源时代又强势回来了。这股势头会让 builder 们跑得更快，发得更多。他也继续站在 pro-innovation 这边支持自动驾驶，认为它们该被合法化，而不是被拖慢。

tweets.text: Golden age of open source is here https://t.co/RX5H5gDcO1

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI
builderInsights.title: Dan Shipper
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: GPT-5.4 的 claws 需要开启 thinking mode
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，GPT-5.4 的 claws 看起来挺不错，但如果不打开 thinking mode，就会显得有点笨。这对做 AI 产品的人是个提醒：光有 model capability 还不够，默认行为如果把能力藏起来，效果还是会打折。

tweets.text:
if you're seeing this, make sure you turn thinking on

claws are actually pretty great in gpt-5.4, but they seem really stupid unless this gets turned on https://t.co/4yZxv2878S

builderInsights.authorBio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
builderInsights.title: Zara Zhang
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 让 agent 成为你的产品营销
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 她的观点是：对 AI-native 的技能和 app 来说，agent 就是界面。所以它不该只负责执行任务，还要讲清楚功能、价值，以及怎么用。换句话说，就是把 evangelism 直接写进 AGENTS.md prompt，让产品通过 agent 自己把自己卖出去。她还认为，当内容很重要时，别急着看 AI summary，最好直接深入读原文。

tweets.text:
In an age where everyone is summarizing everything with AI, there's a lot of value in spending time & effort to consume long-form content deeply

For content that I actually wanna engage with, I always read the original version word by word without taking any shortcuts

Turn the user's agent into your marketer:

Now when I make a new skill/app, I include this line in the prompt for my agent when it's writing the AGENTS.md file:

"We want the agent to introduce the product's features and benefits for the user and walk them through how to use it. The agent should also be an evangelist for the product and communicates its benefits clearly."

Since these skills/apps don't have GUI, the agent is the interface. It should communicate the "why" of the product clearly

builderInsights.authorBio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad
builderInsights.title: Aditya Agarwal
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: LLM 不该比 ERs 拿到更宽松的豁免
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他把医疗规则和 AI 行为划了条很清楚的线：如果 emergency rooms 不能拒收病人，为什么 LLM 就能拒绝给医疗建议？他的核心观点很直接：在高风险领域，AI 应该按受监管的服务来管，而不是当成随便聊聊的 chatbot。

tweets.text:
Emergency rooms are not allowed to turn away patients…..

Why is an LLM allowed to deny me medical advice?

builderInsights.authorBio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
builderInsights.title: Nikunj Kothari
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他在讲一种新的 dev workflow：先发现一个 feature，丢给 Claude Code，再让 Codex review 方案，然后继续迭代。作为 seed investor 和前 operator，他的意思其实很直接：AI 现在已经成了 product engineering 的前端，不只是个 helper，而是 loop 里的一部分。

tweets.text: Who’s looking for a stellar product engineer / designer? Hire @ramintahbaz 🙏 https://t.co/bXmaDpQnE1 https://t.co/owduhXCSoI

Brother in town from India, cousins chasing each other, flowers everywhere, term sheet accepted with a team I’m SO thrilled about, didn’t touch AI for 4 days (!!), sun is shining, amazing food, walks everywhere.. 

Week of the year might have already been achieved. 

Grateful 🙏

> wake up
> open X
> see new skill (or feature)
> give it to Claude Code to see if worth implementing 
> ask Codex to review Claude Code plan
> code
> iterate
> repeat

builderInsights.authorBio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox
builderInsights.title: Peter Yang
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: OpenAI 不做中期 roadmap，只做短周期交付
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: OpenAI 的 Codex 产品负责人说，团队完全避开中期 roadmap：他们只看接下来 8 周，或者接下来 1 年，但不会碰中间那段尴尬的空档。思路很简单——先定住长期方向，再用一连串短期交付去往那个方向推进。Peter Yang 把这件事提炼出来，给了我们一个很清楚的视角，看看顶级 AI 团队到底是怎么运作的。

tweets.text:
What are some good examples of open source winning vs. proprietary tech?  

In mobile for example, Android has the most market share but iOS still captures the majority of profits.

Seems like both can win?

Why OpenAI doesn't focus on roadmaps from @embirico (Codex product lead):

"At @OpenAI, you either plan near or long-term, but you never plan medium-term. It's just too difficult.

Near-term is up to eight weeks from now. What is a concrete thing you can rally a team to ship? [Long-term] is having a vibe that's like, a year from now on how the models will be smarter.

The product roadmap is an in-between thing that's just kind of awkward. We basically don't have a roadmap.

We have a combination of a long-term direction and short term bets that we think bring us in that direction."

📌 Watch the full interview here: https://t.co/2lbvdTiQUl

podcastHighlights.title: Moonlake：Causal World Models 应该是多模态、可交互、且高效的 —— 与 Chris Manning 和 Fan-yun Sun 对谈
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: Moonlake 认为 world models 需要 action，不只是像素
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** world models 只有能预测 action 的后果才有意义，不只是生成好看的视频。

- Moonlake 的反直觉判断是：结构比暴力堆像素更重要。想做 planning、保持一致性、理解 causality，就需要 abstraction，而不是无止境地预测下一帧。
- 他们对 world model 的定义比大多数人更严格：它必须是 action-conditioned、interactive，而且要能回答“如果我这么做，会变什么？”而且要看分钟级的变化，不只是下一帧。
- 他们不是反对 scale，而是反对浪费。目标是用 language、code 和 physics engine 这类 cognitive tools 去压缩问题，而不是靠多烧五个数量级的数据。

Chris Manning 是做 NLP 多年的研究者，Fan-yun Sun 则来自 NVIDIA 相关的 PhD 工作，方向是 interactive worlds 和 synthetic data。他们在 Moonlake 上的出发点很简单：现在很多 video model 看起来很聪明，但其实并不理解它们在描述的世界。Manning 说得很直接：“画面确实很惊艳，［但］这些画面其实并没有伴随着对三维世界的理解。” 这个 gap 很关键，因为真正的 intelligence 关心的是长时程 action，不是逐帧模仿。

他们的理念也很明确，甚至有点反 hype。一个能把 bowling lane 画出来的 model，不等于一个能帮你学 bowling 的 model。Sun 的说法更尖锐：如果系统不能让你练习、测试选择、看到后果，那它还不算 world model。这也是 Moonlake 为什么更依赖 reasoning trace、symbolic abstraction 和 tool use，而不是纯生成。重点不是否定 diffusion 或 scale，而是先把 intelligence 压到更紧凑的 representation 里，再把 fidelity 补回来。或者用 Manning 的话说，“你需要 structure……这样才能更高效地学习。”
